title: Worked Example - Multiple Level Inheritance in C++

content.problemStatement:
In this example, we will demonstrate how **Multiple Level Inheritance** works in C++. This involves a **grandparent class**, a **parent class**, and a **child class**, where each class builds on the properties and methods of the previous class.

We will use an **Animal** class (Grandparent) which is inherited by a **Mammal** class (Parent), and the **Dog** class (Child) inherits from the **Mammal** class.

Each class adds its own specific properties and methods, and the child class can access members from both the parent and grandparent classes.

---

**When executed, the code will show:**

```default
Animal Characteristics
Species: Canine
Mammal Characteristics
Has Fur: Yes
Dog Characteristics
Breed: Golden Retriever
```
